does: CodePMP introduces a scalable preference model pretraining pipeline that addresses the data scarcity bottleneck in reward model training for LLMs. The method synthesizes preference pairs from publicly available high-quality source code and uses these pairs to pretrain preference models before finetuning on downstream tasks. The approach evaluates effectiveness on both mathematical reasoning benchmarks (GSM8K, MATH) and logical reasoning tasks (ReClor, LogiQA2.0), demonstrating consistent improvements in LLM reasoning performance while reducing the annotation burden typically required for RLHF-based reward model development.
connects: This work builds on the established pipeline of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) for LLM improvement, which has become standard practice following InstructGPT and subsequent models. It extends recent research on scaling preference learning and reward modeling by recognizing that high-quality preference data remains the critical constraint. CodePMP connects to prior work on data synthesis and transfer learning in NLP, where pretraining on related domains improves downstream performance, as well as to broader efforts in leveraging code as a rich supervisory signal for language model training, following successful applications of code-based objectives in models like Codex.
enables: This work opens pathways for more efficient RLHF pipelines by demonstrating that preference models can effectively transfer knowledge from code domains. It suggests broader applications of synthetic preference pair generation from other structured, high-quality domains beyond code. The approach points toward reducing human annotation costs in reward modeling, potentially enabling more frequent model alignment updates and more widespread adoption of RLHF for specialized reasoning tasks. Future work could explore combining CodePMP with other preference learning objectives, extending synthesis strategies to other domains, and investigating whether preference model pretraining benefits other alignment techniques beyond traditional RLHF.
